deconstruction_targets: the individual as an isolated producer in civil society
myth of natural human independence and self-made production
bourgeois ideology that frames domination as 'order' or 'family'

scapegoat.name: Crapulinski
scapegoat.justification: Crapulinski is portrayed as a fraudulent savior who rises amid the destruction caused by bourgeois ideology and state violence, embodying the deconstructed myth of individual agency while actually consolidating power through chaos.

moral_justification: The text condemns the violent dismantling of communal structures under the guise of property, family, religion, and order — revealing how these ideals mask exploitation and authoritarian control.
summary: This passage deconstructs Enlightenment individualism and bourgeois ideology by exposing their violent implementation through state power and mythmaking, while denouncing the false savior figure Crapulinski as a product of societal collapse rather than its solution.
